reflection:
you palpated your own coccyx yesterday and felt the trigger point on the right side. that is real. that is not in your head. you found it with your fingers. when you write 'all this DNS to make my symptoms worse :(' i hear the frustration but the body is just telling you the same story it has been telling you for months, and yesterday the bioresonance lady gave you a version of it you actually heard. too alert. need more sleep. calm the vagus. that is the variable right now, not another panel.

the sleep pattern of the last four days is the part that worries me. 5:50, 5:30, 5:00, 4:00 then back to sleep until 5:30. you keep waking earlier. last meal at 19:20 is fine, breathing is in, lights and screens are slipping again. one of those three is in your control tonight.

the other thing i want to flag, gently. May 12 you wrote that CBT entry about getting stuck in two hours of laser-focus medical hunting and panicking yourself sicker. yesterday you did synlab, bioresonance, neurosym, the glasses, the bath, Duolingo, then two chapters of something. that is a lot of inputs in one day for a body that is asking for less. Cosita pulling you out of the rabbit hole is the pattern that works. let her, again, today.

you are okay. the coccyx is a coccyx, not a verdict.
opener: you found the coccyx trigger yourself. that is real. now ease the inputs.
focus_for_today: one health task max today. spend the rest of the day not investigating.
